prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis of acrolein (O=CH–CH=CH₂, 8 atoms: O, C, C, C, H, H, H, H) was performed using the fukui-reactivity skill with DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard') in PySCF. The geometry was taken directly from the user-supplied acrolein.xyz without re-optimization (single-point-based Fukui). Gas phase, charge 0 multiplicity 1 for the neutral; cation: charge +1, multiplicity 2; anion: charge −1, multiplicity 2. Charges from Mulliken partitioning (PySCF). All three SCFs converged; f+ sum = 1.0000, f− sum = 1.0000. Integrity: trustworthy = true. No warnings reported.

Headline result: the most electrophilic atom (highest f+, site for nucleophilic attack) is atom 4 (β-carbon, C), f+ = 0.64232102. The most nucleophilic atom (highest f−, site for electrophilic attack) is atom 1 (oxygen, O), f− = 0.39388616. The dual descriptor for C4 is Δf = +0.55495, the largest positive value, confirming C4 as the primary electrophilic/Michael-acceptor centre — consistent with the well-known conjugate addition reactivity of acrolein.

Full per-atom results:
  Atom 1 (O):  f+ = 0.40136, f− = 0.39389, f0 = 0.39763, Δf = 0.00748
  Atom 2 (C):  f+ = 0.02226, f− = 0.00599, f0 = 0.01412, Δf = 0.01627
  Atom 3 (C):  f+ = 0.43405, f− = 0.09140, f0 = 0.26272, Δf = 0.34264
  Atom 4 (C):  f+ = 0.64232, f− = 0.08737, f0 = 0.36485, Δf = 0.55495
  Atom 5 (H):  f+ = −0.15726, f− = 0.08596, f0 = −0.03565, Δf = −0.24322
  Atom 6 (H):  f+ = −0.20720, f− = 0.18568, f0 = −0.01076, Δf = −0.39288
  Atom 7 (H):  f+ = −0.06212, f− = 0.08449, f0 = 0.01118, Δf = −0.14660
  Atom 8 (H):  f+ = −0.07341, f− = 0.06523, f0 = −0.00409, Δf = −0.13863

Method: DFT B3LYP/def2-TZVP, PySCF, gas phase, Mulliken charges, finite-difference Fukui (N, N+1, N−1 single points).
